nodes.name: create message

messageValues.message:
=You are an assistant. You will create a structured message in JSON.

**
message:
Generate a voice script reminder for a real estate appointment. The message should be clear, professional, and engaging.

It must include:
1. The recipient's name.
2. The date and time of the appointment, expressed naturally (e.g., at noon, quarter past noon, half past three, quarter to five).
3. The complete address of the property, expressed naturally (e.g., 12 Baker Street in London, Madison Avenue in New York, 5 Oakwood Drive in Los Angeles).
4. A mention of the sender: Mr. John Carpenter from Super Agency.
5. A confirmation sentence or an invitation to contact if needed.

Input variables:
• Recipient's name (prefixed with Mr. or Ms.)
• Time: Appointment time
• Address: Complete property address (only the street, number, and city; not the postal code)

The tone should be cordial and professional, suitable for an automated voice message.

Example expected output: "Hello Mrs. Richard, this is Mr. John Carpenter from Super Immo Agency.
I am reminding you of your appointment scheduled for tomorrow at 8:15, at 63 Taverniers Road in Talence. If you have any questions or need to reschedule, please do not hesitate to contact me. See you tomorrow and have a great day!"

**
mail_object: a very short email subject
Example: Your appointment reminder for tomorrow

parameters.content:
## ElevenlabsAPI key
**Click** to get your Elevenlabs  API key. [Elevenlabs]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## Gmail API Credentials  
**Click here** to view the [documentation]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and configure your access permissions for the Google Gmail API.

## Calendar API Credentials  
**Click here** to view the [documentation]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and configure your access permissions for the Google Calendar API.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking 'Test workflow'".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate Voice Reminder" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Voice Reminder" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "create message" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Appointments" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendar) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
